presentation.title: Welcome to SPIKE — Venture Incubator Orientation

slides.title: Welcome to S.P.I.K.E.
slides.body:
SPIKE is a Startup Business Incubator — not a traditional internship. You are building a 3-Year business blueprint as a financial entrepreneur.

Stakeholders: Interns (venture builders), Advisory Board (coaches), Venture Partners (agency directors).
slides.speakerNotes: Open with energy. Emphasize incubator language — avoid 'classroom' framing. 5 minutes.
discussionQuestions: What surprised you most about the incubator model?
How is this different from internships you have seen before?

slides.title: Program Architecture
slides.body:
Segment 1 (200 hours) → Proof of Concept Venture Board.

Every day contributes to: Portfolio, Business Plan, Competencies, and Venture readiness.

Week 1 focus: Vision & Purpose — Identity & Purpose portfolio section.
slides.speakerNotes: Walk through the six venture-incubator dimensions. Point to Playbook navigation they will use daily.
discussionQuestions: Which output system — portfolio or business plan — feels most unfamiliar today?

slides.title: Squad Dynamics
slides.body:
Research Squads (3–4): market intelligence and collaborative problem solving.

Advisory Dyads: roleplay, mock client meetings, pitch refinement.

Faculty coaches assign squads today.
slides.speakerNotes: Explain squad charter activity coming next. Dyads pair by complementary strengths.
discussionQuestions: What strength will you bring to your research squad?
What do you want from your advisory dyad partner?

slides.title: Day 1 Outputs
slides.body:
By end of day:
• Personal why statement (worksheet)
• Squad charter (activity)
• Orientation reflection → Portfolio: Identity & Purpose
• Vision narrative seed → Business Plan Chapter 1
slides.speakerNotes: Close with clear deliverables. Link to competency targets: Visioning and Goal Setting.
discussionQuestions: What is one goal you will hold yourself accountable to before Day 2?
